title: astrebla triticoides var. lappacea
intro: Astrebla triticoides var. lappacea, also known as Button Grass, is a type of grass native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive button-like spikelets. Traditionally, the plant has been used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective against wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Button Grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or poultice, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
